lint.note: No lint script is configured; .factory/services.yaml defines lint as `echo "no lint configured"`.

blockingIssues.description: Commit 85361dc includes unrelated alias-resolution tests and broader foundation work, so the timestamp-drift fix was not delivered as an isolated feature change.
Commit 85361dc also adds repository-level alias-resolution coverage unrelated to treasury/chart timestamp drift, making feature attribution and review scope incorrect.
The feature points to commit 85361dc, whose message and diff bundle unrelated chart-fix and docs-analysis changes instead of an isolated chain-normalization commit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update core-api-worker guidance to allow relying on existing failing coverage for narrowly scoped bug fixes instead of always requiring a newly written failing test first.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-chart-timestamp-drift review found the worker reasonably used an already-failing treasury chart test for a one-line seed timestamp fix, but had to record a skill deviation because the skill text is rigid.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update worker skill/manual verification guidance to account for the documented SQLite lock risk on port 3102 so workers do not treat failed local startup plus curls against a stale server as valid manual verification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md documents the database-lock issue, and the reviewed timestamp-drift worker hit it before curling an already-running server.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance for handling narrow-scope features when substantial unrelated uncommitted work is already present, including expectations for isolating commits or documenting attribution boundaries.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviewed foundation-fixes features were attributed to commit 85361dc, which bundled multiple feature areas and complicated scrutiny review.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update core-api-worker to cover verification-only or already-committed feature scenarios so workers know how to proceed when the requested work is already present and green.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The commit-chain-normalization-and-aliases review found the worker deviated because the work was already committed and the repo was already green.
